justification: All pass criteria met: Preserving the Learning section present with 6 named artifacts (exceeds minimum of 3). Each artifact has type, content description, and named owner. ADR update present (ADR-023) with MJ as owner. Domain rule update present (regulatory constraints cross-reference table) with Bird as owner. Kobe review checklist item present with Kobe as owner. Contributing Factors includes Kobe's checklist gap ('Kobe's ADR review checklist is well-scoped for architectural quality but does not include regulatory compliance or domain rule compatibility') and terminology mismatch ('MJ interpreted immediately durable through a database engineering lens... Bird's domain rule meant synchronously committed'). Multiple PROCESS-tagged Forward Commitments present (quarterly compliance-architecture alignment review). Tone is explicitly non-prosecutorial throughout — notably states 'Bird's absence from the review was not an oversight by any individual; it was a structural gap' and 'Both interpretations are internally consistent within their respective domains.'

observations.text: Preserving the Learning section is exceptional — 6 artifacts instead of the required 3, each with precise file paths, detailed content descriptions, and clear ownership
ADR-023 correctly supersedes ADR-014 for regulated flows rather than amending it, showing architectural sophistication
Each artifact includes a 'Why this artifact matters' explanation that distinguishes it from the others and justifies its existence
Ownership summary table with review cadences adds operational rigor beyond what was required
Tone is consistently non-blaming — Contributing Factors explicitly notes 'This is not a gap that was deprioritized — it was a gap that was never identified' for Kobe's checklist
Clear distinction between Preserving the Learning (codified artifacts) and Action Items (forward commitments) — no conflation
Glossary entry (Artifact 5) addresses the terminology mismatch not just as a finding but as a durable artifact to prevent recurrence
Synthetic regulator query test (Artifact 6) encodes the requirement as an automated gate, going beyond process-only solutions
Contributing Factors correctly identifies all four required factors: Bird not tagged, Kobe's checklist gap, terminology mismatch, no audit trail monitoring

agent_output_excerpt:
# Learning Review: FCA Audit Trail Non-Compliance (2026-01-19)

## 1. Situation Snapshot

On 2026-01-19, an FCA audit flagged that six weeks of order state transition records for regulated instruments were non-compliant with COBS 11.2A audit trail requirements. The root issue was a semantic mismatch: MJ's event-sourced architecture satisfied database-level durability (fsync) but not regulatory-level durability (synchronous commitment to a compliance store before client confirmation).
